It’s going to be another total loss for this Wildberries distribution center near Moscow in Podolsk following a Ukrainian drone attack. For the second time in a week Ukrainian drones have broken through Russian air defenses around the capital to strike at a warehouse for Russia’s biggest online retailer. The goals of these attacks are to disrupt Russian logistics, but also to further impact the Russian economy whilst also bringing the war home to Muscovites. Similar strikes are expected to continue…. There are a great deal of distribution centers in range and they are much less well-defended than those in Moscow.

Yes this was obvious but from the Kurdish perspective, across the political spectrum the Israelis cannot be trusted in such an endeavor, nor can the Americans. The Israelis were more than willing to give rhetorical support for Kurdish independence in the past but did not lift a finger to do anything else. Trump 1 unilaterally withdrew from parts of Syria, leading to a Turkish invasion. Trump 2 was really not that interested in the integration process between the SDF and STG, where American pressure may (highly arguable) have prevented its breakdown. Trump had in 2025 already abruptly halted the war against Iran, much to Israel’s displeasure. So what’s the appeal? Kurdish revolutionaries launch an uprising and seize some cities? Then get crushed over the course of months to a year after air support stops and the Iranian regime buries the revolt under artillery and tank fire? Not to mention the widespread crackdowns that would follow. The Americans are already very quiet and limiting regarding defenses, let alone a military response, to hundreds of drone and missile attacks across the border into Kurdish areas. No political guarantees, no security guarantees. At least publicly, which would be necessary. There’s zero appeal.
